title: Beyond trace-class and Hilbert-Schmidt -- Interaction between operator ideals and von Neumann algebras in quantum physics
abstract: Starting from a thorough analysis of the conjugate $\overline{H}$ of a complex Hilbert space $H$, including its significant importance regarding a representation of the tensor product of two complex Hilbert spaces and its impact to the theorem of Fréchet-Riesz over to a revisit of applications of nuclear and absolutely $p$-summing operators in algebraic quantum field theory (AQFT) in the sense of Araki, Haag and Kastler ($p=2$) and more recently in the framework of general probabilistic spaces ($p=1$), we will outline that Banach operator ideals in the sense of Pietsch, or equivalently tensor products of Banach spaces in the sense of Grothendieck are even lurking in the foundations and philosophy of quantum physics and quantum information theory. In particular, we concentrate on their importance in AQFT (Theorem 5.27). In doing so, we revisit the role of trace-class operators in quantum theory and construct the enveloping $\tup{C}^\adj$-algebra, corresponding to an arbitrarily given normed operator ideal (Proposition 5.3 and Theorem 5.5).   Applications are presented, including a purely linear algebraic description of the quantum teleportation process, thereby showing a link to quantum information theory, also due to the emergence of the Hadamard-Walsh transform and the controlled NOT gate (Example 4.18).   All Hilbert spaces discussed in this paper may be nonseparable (and hence infinite-dimensional).
summary: This theoretical paper bridges functional analysis and quantum physics by showing that concepts like operator ideals are fundamental to quantum theory. It rigorously connects the tensor product of Hilbert spaces to specific classes of bounded operators, offering a deep mathematical insight into quantum information and quantum field theory structures.
main_problem: The paper aims to reveal deep, structural connections between abstract mathematical tools—specifically operator ideals (like trace-class and $p$-nuclear operators)—and the foundational formalism of quantum physics, including AQFT and quantum information theory.
main_result: It establishes that the tensor product structure of Hilbert spaces can be rigorously identified with spaces of absolutely $p$-summing operators, providing a linear algebraic description for processes like quantum teleportation.
method: The methodology combines advanced functional analysis (Pietsch's operator ideals, Grothendieck theory) with the mathematical framework of von Neumann algebras and the analysis of conjugate Hilbert spaces.
model_or_system: The theoretical framework involves general complex Hilbert spaces, their tensor products, and the algebraic structures underpinning quantum field theories (von Neumann algebras). Applications include modeling quantum teleportation and analyzing compatibility in General Probabilistic Theories (GPTs).
key_observables: Operator ideals ($\mathcal{L}_2, \mathcal{N}, \mathcal{P}_2$), the compatibility degree $\gamma$, and the modular operator $\Delta$.
important_parameters: The parameter $p$ in $p$-nuclear operators ($1 \le p < \infty$), and the number of measurements $g$ in GPTs.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies heavily on advanced theorems regarding isometric isomorphisms between a Hilbert space and its dual, and the assumption of 'split' properties for commuting von Neumann algebras.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from analyzing the conjugate Hilbert space and its relation to the dual space, to establishing the identification of tensor products with operator ideals, and finally applying these concepts to AQFT and GPTs.
why_interesting: It provides a highly abstract, mathematical underpinning for quantum information protocols (like teleportation) and quantum field theory, suggesting that the underlying mathematical structure is governed by operator ideal theory.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
